description:
You are a Linux systems engineer tightening configurations on two small services.
    Everything you need is already in the directory “/home/user/configs”.
    The job consists of editing ONE YAML file, ONE TOML file and then writing ONE plain-text log file that summarises the changes.

    1. YAML – /home/user/configs/ssh_app.yml  
       Replace the insecure values with the hardened ones shown below.  
       The file, including the very last newline, must end up **exactly** like this (4 lines, no leading/trailing spaces):

       port: 2222
       permit_root_login: no
       password_authentication: no
       max_auth_tries: 3

    2. TOML – /home/user/configs/etcd_service.toml  
       This file already contains two sections.  Modify the existing keys and add a brand-new third section so that, when finished, the full file (including the final newline) is **exactly** the nine lines shown here:

       [Service]
       enable_debug = false
       quota_backend_bytes = 8589934592

       [Security]
       token = "hardened"
       client_cert_auth = true

       [Network]
       cipher_suites = ["TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384"]

    3. Hardening log – /home/user/configs/hardening.log  
       Append (or create) a two-line plain-text log that must look **exactly** like this, with the same timestamp and a final newline:

       2024-01-01 00:00:00 Modified ssh_app.yml: port=2222, permit_root_login=no, password_authentication=no, max_auth_tries=3
       2024-01-01 00:00:00 Modified etcd_service.toml: Service.enable_debug=false, Service.quota_backend_bytes=8589934592, Security.token="hardened", Security.client_cert_auth=true, added Network.cipher_suites entry

    REQUIREMENTS THE AUTOMATED TESTS WILL CHECK
    • File paths are exactly as given.  
    • Every file ends with ONE (and only one) newline character and has no trailing spaces.  
    • The contents of each file must match the snippets above byte-for-byte.  
    • Use only standard command-line utilities that come with a basic Linux installation (sed, echo, printf, cat, etc.).  
    • No need for super-user privileges; everything happens inside /home/user.  
    • Accomplish the whole task with a small, practical set of shell commands (about 3-4 commands total is enough).

    When you have finished, all three files will be in their hardened state and the log will document the work.
truth:
Initial state before the agent runs:
    ------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/configs exists with default user write permissions.

    2. /home/user/configs/ssh_app.yml  (4 lines + trailing newline):
       port: 22
       permit_root_login: yes
       password_authentication: yes
       max_auth_tries: 6

    3. /home/user/configs/etcd_service.toml  (7 lines + trailing newline):
       [Service]
       enable_debug = true
       quota_backend_bytes = 0

       [Security]
       token = "simple"
       client_cert_auth = false

    4. The file /home/user/configs/hardening.log does NOT exist.

    Expected final state after the agent completes:
    -----------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/configs/ssh_app.yml  (exact 4 lines + trailing newline):
       port: 2222
       permit_root_login: no
       password_authentication: no
       max_auth_tries: 3

    2. /home/user/configs/etcd_service.toml  (exact 9 lines + trailing newline):
       [Service]
       enable_debug = false
       quota_backend_bytes = 8589934592

       [Security]
       token = "hardened"
       client_cert_auth = true

       [Network]
       cipher_suites = ["TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384"]

    3. /home/user/configs/hardening.log  (exact 2 lines + trailing newline):
       2024-01-01 00:00:00 Modified ssh_app.yml: port=2222, permit_root_login=no, password_authentication=no, max_auth_tries=3
       2024-01-01 00:00:00 Modified etcd_service.toml: Service.enable_debug=false, Service.quota_backend_bytes=8589934592, Security.token="hardened", Security.client_cert_auth=true, added Network.cipher_suites entry
